_comment: Windows bug check (BSOD) code catalog. Used by the crashdumps module to translate a raw code from a minidump into a human-readable meaning and to rank likely causes. Fields: code (hex string, uppercase), name (SCREAMING_SNAKE Microsoft canonical name), meaning (short one-liner), commonCauses (ordered by frequency), hardwareVsDriver (hint for triage). See knowledge_base/session_2026-07-04_lenovo_slim7prox.md for a real-world triage example.

codes.name: DPC_WATCHDOG_VIOLATION
codes.meaning: A DPC (Deferred Procedure Call) exceeded its time allowance. Almost always a driver spinning too long in kernel context.
commonCauses: Storage driver stall (older Intel RST, SSD firmware bug, NVMe timeout)
Network driver stall (Realtek WLAN, especially with PCIe ASPM interactions)
GPU driver TDR that manifests as DPC timeout instead of 0x116

codes.name: KERNEL_SECURITY_CHECK_FAILURE
codes.meaning: The kernel detected corruption of a critical data structure — commonly a control-flow guard (GUARD_ICALL) violation or a stack cookie mismatch.
commonCauses: Driver bug corrupting a kernel structure (Realtek WLAN drivers historically frequent offenders)

codes.name: SYSTEM_THREAD_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED
codes.meaning: A system thread raised an exception the handler didn't catch.
commonCauses: Faulting .sys named in parameters — usually a driver bug

codes.name: IRQL_NOT_LESS_OR_EQUAL
codes.meaning: Kernel-mode code accessed pageable memory (or invalid memory) at DISPATCH_LEVEL or higher.
commonCauses: Bad driver dereferencing a null / freed pointer

codes.name: PAGE_FAULT_IN_NONPAGED_AREA
codes.meaning: A page fault in memory that should not have been paged out.

codes.name: KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED
codes.meaning: Kernel-mode program raised an exception the OS did not catch.
commonCauses: Driver bug — check the module named in parameter 1

codes.name: WHEA_UNCORRECTABLE_ERROR
codes.meaning: Hardware raised an uncorrectable machine check exception (MCE). The CPU / chipset / RAM / bus reported it, not Windows.
commonCauses: Failing CPU or CPU voltage instability (undervolt too aggressive)
Failing RAM (uncorrectable ECC or DIMM failure)
Bad PCIe device (rare — link training failure)

codes.name: SPECIAL_POOL_DETECTED_MEMORY_CORRUPTION
codes.meaning: Driver Verifier's special pool caught a driver writing outside its allocation.
commonCauses: Driver bug — the faulting module is named
Only appears when Driver Verifier is active

codes.name: DRIVER_POWER_STATE_FAILURE
codes.meaning: A driver failed to complete a power state transition (sleep/resume/hibernate).
commonCauses: Storage driver stall during sleep transition
USB device that didn't respond to selective suspend
Modern Standby (S0ix) resume failures on Ryzen 6000+ platforms

codes.name: VIDEO_TDR_FAILURE
codes.meaning: The Video TDR (Timeout Detection and Recovery) fired and the recovery failed. GPU didn't respond in time.

codes.name: MEMORY_MANAGEMENT
codes.meaning: The memory manager detected an invariant violation.
